title: Cults, Sex, and the American Dream in 'Wild Wild Country' I The Big Picture (Ep. 56)
format: Sean Fennessey talks to Chapman and McClain Way and producer Mark Duplass about Wild Wild Country, their Netflix docuseries on the Rajneeshpuram commune: 300 hours of unseen archive, five days spent with Ma Anand Sheela, the land-use and church-and-state fights buried under the guns and the sex, and Duplass admitting he half-envies the idealism.

segments.heading: The Way brothers and Mark Duplass on building a cult on Netflix
segments.guest: Chapman Way, McClain Way and Mark Duplass on

films.title: The Battered Bastards of Baseball
films.note: The Ways' only prior film, a 72-minute doc about a forgotten Portland baseball team, financed themselves and picked up by Netflix out of Sundance. They keep returning to it as the small credit that made Wild Wild Country a hard sell to interview subjects and to buyers alike.

segments.heading: The last great thing they've seen

picks.title: Call Me by Your Name
picks.note: Watched it on a flight to Copenhagen; says the father-son scene wrecked him for the rest of the trip.

picks.title: Before Sunrise
picks.note: Watched the whole Before trilogy in a row while sequestered with strep throat; a huge Linklater fan, he came out of it reassured he was in the right line of work.

picks.title: Before Midnight
picks.note: The one that closed out his binge.

TV: The Zen Diaries of Gary Shandling
